forecasts.reasoning: Based on available evidence up to April 29, 2025, there has been no official declaration of candidacy by Alassane Ouattara, and the RHDP ruling party "has yet to nominate its candidate" as of April 23, 2025. While there is strong party pressure for him to run (RHDP affirmed him as their "obvious candidate" in May 2024, and ministers stated he "has no choice but to accept"), and Ouattara himself said in January 2025 he "would like to continue serving," he also stated he had "not yet decided" and would only run if his party backed his candidacy. Given only about 1 month remains before the June 1 deadline, no imminent indication of a declaration, his age (83), his history of waffling on this decision, and the lack of formal nomination as of late April, the probability of a declaration before June 1, 2025 appears moderate but below 50%. I estimate approximately 35% - there's a real possibility he could announce, but the short timeline and lack of current momentum weigh against it.
